title: Fix the Binary Search Bug
description: The following binary search implementation has a bug that causes it to loop infinitely or return incorrect results. Find and fix the bug.
problemStatement: Given a sorted array of integers and a target value, implement binary search to return the index of the target, or -1 if not found. The current implementation is broken — identify and fix the issue.
hints: Check the boundary conditions for left and right pointers
What happens when left equals right?
The mid pointer update is causing infinite loop

title: Implement a Rate Limiter
description: Implement a token bucket rate limiter class that allows a maximum of N requests per time window.
problemStatement:
Build a RateLimiter class with the following interface:
- constructor(maxRequests, windowMs): Initialize with max requests and time window in ms
- isAllowed(userId): Returns true if the request is allowed, false if rate limited

The limiter should track requests per user independently.

visibleTests.input: new RateLimiter(3, 1000); allow 3 requests from user1
new RateLimiter(3, 1000); allow 4 requests from user1

hiddenTests.input: Different users should have independent limits
Requests outside window should not count

hints: Use a Map to track per-user request timestamps
Filter out timestamps older than the window
Sliding window approach works well here

title: Flatten Nested Object
description: Write a function that flattens a deeply nested object into a single-level object with dot-notation keys.
problemStatement: Given a nested object like { a: { b: { c: 1 } }, d: 2 }, return { 'a.b.c': 1, 'd': 2 }. Handle arrays by using index notation like 'arr.0.key'.
hints: Track the current key path as a prefix
Base case: value is not an object
